regions.us-ports:
US Ports Region Weather Briefing for April 06, 2026:

Conditions are generally mild with light to moderate winds across the East Coast ports. Charleston reports overcast skies with northwest winds at 7 knots and a low 11 percent chance of rain, trending to light drizzle and slightly stronger northeast winds by Day 1. Mariners in Charleston Harbor should prepare for reduced visibility in drizzle and maintain caution with NNE winds increasing to 14 knots. Elsewhere, expect mostly overcast conditions with light NE winds up to 18 knots along the Southeast and Mid-Atlantic coasts; no marine wind advisories are in effect. West Coast ports including Long Beach, Los Angeles, San Diego, and San Francisco will experience stable conditions with light onshore breezes and minimal precipitation. Cruise operators and yacht captains should monitor localized drizzle near Charleston and plan for smooth sailing elsewhere, while fishing vessels along the Southeast should secure gear for moderate northeast winds on Days 2 and 3.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Bahamas and Central Caribbean briefing for April 6, 2026: Nassau currently enjoys clear skies with light ENE winds at 5 knots and a low 2% chance of rain. Expect overcast conditions by afternoon with easterly winds increasing to around 9 knots and a slight rain chance near 10%, rising significantly to 50% plus on April 7 and 8 with southerly to southeast winds 9-10 knots and drizzle developing. Mariners should prepare for reduced visibility and wet decks later in the week, especially near Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands where similar conditions are expected. Fishing captains and yacht operators are advised to monitor local forecasts closely and maintain readiness for light rain and overcast skies; port operations at Key West, Grand Cayman, and Santo Domingo remain normal with no marine warnings in effect.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for April 6, 2026, shows light ESE winds at 5 knots and partly cloudy skies with no rain in San Juan. Expect increasing ESE winds to 11 knots and scattered rain showers through the next two days, with a 40% chance today and 39% tomorrow, mainly impacting open waters and exposed anchorages around San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten. Thunderstorm chances drop to 11% on day three but remain possible, so maintain vigilance for sudden gusts or brief squalls, especially near St. Kitts and Antigua. Vessel operators should monitor sky conditions closely, secure loose gear, and advise passengers to stay clear of decks during showers and potential thunderstorms.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean Outlook for April 6, 2026: Cozumel currently reports 78°F with northeast winds at 7 knots under overcast skies and no immediate rain threat. Over the next three days, expect northeast to north winds 8-11 knots with increasing rain shower chances rising to 35-38%, tapering to 25% by day three. Conditions remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, but fishing captains should monitor intermittent showers and reduced visibility, especially near Belize City and Roatan anchorages. Cruise passengers are advised to carry rain gear during port calls at Costa Maya and Cozumel and remain cautious of slippery decks during rain showers.
regions.southern-caribbean:
Southern Caribbean Weather Briefing for April 6, 2026:

Trade winds remain steady from the east at 17-20 knots across Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena, with overcast skies dominating and minimal rain chances (under 5%) through the next three days. Sea conditions will be moderate; expect occasional 4-6 foot waves near open waters but no Small Craft Advisories at this time. Yacht and fishing captains should secure light gear topside due to steady easterly gusts near 20 knots, especially when operating near offshore anchorages around Aruba and Bonaire. Cruise passengers can anticipate mild temperatures in the low 80s with overcast skies, ideal for daytime excursions but advise light windbreakers for breezy conditions on decks.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for April 6, 2026: Barbados reports steady east winds at 17 knots with overcast skies and a low 20 percent chance of showers this morning. Expect continued easterly trade winds 16-18 knots regionwide with mostly overcast conditions and isolated light rain, highest near Barbados and St. Lucia. Seas remain moderate; no Small Craft Advisories or warnings in effect. Yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear and monitor local radar for brief showers, especially near Barbados and Martinique anchorages where visibility may briefly drop. Cruise passengers should plan for intermittent clouds and light rain but no significant weather disruptions today.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and surrounding ports will experience light westerly winds at 2 to 12 knots today with overcast skies and minimal rain chances under 1 percent. Conditions remain stable for vessels operating out of Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island with calm seas and no advisories in effect. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor westerly winds increasing to 12 knots this afternoon, especially nearshore, while cruise passengers can expect mild temperatures near 71 degrees with overcast skies. Expect a gradual wind shift to southerly 8 to 9 knots over the next two days with steady overcast conditions and no precipitation.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions for April 6, 2026: current temperature is 59°F with light WNW winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies; rain chances remain negligible at 1 percent. Winds will increase WSW to 10 knots today under overcast skies, easing slightly over the next two days with continued mild WSW to SW breezes and no precipitation expected. Mariners should expect calm seas, favorable for transit and anchorage at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Fishing and yacht captains can plan for stable conditions with light to moderate winds, but remain vigilant for any sudden weather changes given the overcast conditions on days one and two.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports including San Jose del Cabo and La Paz will experience mild conditions today with clear skies, light NNW winds at 6 knots, and temperatures near 74°F. Over the next three days, expect overcast skies with NW winds steady between 8 and 12 knots, maintaining a dry forecast with no rain expected. Mariners should find calm seas suitable for small craft operations, though fishing captains should monitor the gradual increase in wind speed by Day 3, especially when operating near open anchorage areas off San Jose del Cabo. Passengers on shore excursions can plan outdoor activities confidently with no precipitation or severe weather threats in the period ahead.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions for April 6, 2026 remain stable with light WNW winds at 1 knot, 78 degrees Fahrenheit, and partly cloudy skies. Expect steady west to southwest winds increasing to 8-10 knots over the next three days with overcast conditions but no rain. Mariners should note calm seas near Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, suitable for safe anchoring and fishing operations. Cruise and yacht captains should monitor for gradual wind shifts but anticipate no significant weather disruptions; passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with minimal weather impact.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is experiencing calm and clear conditions this morning with light northerly winds at 3 knots and temperatures near 80 degrees Fahrenheit. Over the next three days, expect moderate westerly winds increasing to 10 knots, with overcast skies through day two and partial clearing by day three. No precipitation or marine wind warnings are forecast, making it favorable for cruising, fishing, and yacht operations. Mariners should remain vigilant for reduced visibility in overcast conditions especially near Punta Mita and Yelapa anchorages, and plan for gentle seas and steady westerly breezes when navigating coastal waters.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean outlook for April 6, 2026: Barcelona currently reports light westerly winds at 2 knots with clear skies and temperatures near 60°F, with no rain expected. Over the next three days, expect mild conditions with light variable winds, shifting from south-southwest to northeast, and increasing cloud cover by day two and three, but no precipitation. Ports including Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar should see similarly stable weather with light winds and no marine warnings. Yacht and fishing captains should anticipate calm seas and good visibility; however, monitor the gradual increase in overcast conditions for potential changes in local wind patterns, especially near coastal anchorages. Passengers can enjoy comfortable days on deck with minimal weather disruptions.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean conditions for April 6, 2026 remain stable with light southerly winds and mostly clear skies. Naples reports 60°F, S 4 kt, with no rain and excellent visibility, ideal for port operations and recreational activities. Over the next three days, expect moderate southerly breezes (4-8 kt) with partly cloudy to foggy conditions developing overnight near Naples and southern ports such as Civitavecchia and La Spezia. Yacht captains should maintain caution entering fog-prone anchorages and use radar and AIS for safe navigation, especially around Venice and Dubrovnik where early morning fog may reduce visibility. Fishing vessels will find calm seas and favorable conditions for nearshore operations throughout the region.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean briefing for April 6, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) reports mild conditions with clear skies, light WNW winds at 3 knots, and no rain. Expect a gradual warming trend through the region, with winds shifting from north to south over the next two days, accompanied by patchy fog in ports such as Santorini and Mykonos on April 7. Rhodes, Cyprus, and Izmir will see mostly stable, light winds under 10 knots, suitable for smooth navigation and anchorage. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor the increasing overcast and 23% chance of rain on April 8, especially near coastal areas like Haifa and Alexandria, and prepare for reduced visibility and possible light showers. Passengers should plan outdoor activities for today and tomorrow, but carry rain gear for late in the period.
